title: 3D-Imaging and Quantitative Subsurface Dielectric Constant Measurement Using Peak Force Kelvin Probe Force Microscopy
author: KHALED KAJA
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: We demonstrate a new approach to simultaneously measure the dielectric constants of buried structures and interfaces by combining peak force tapping quantitative Nano-mechanical mapping (PF QNM) and frequency-modulated Kelvin probe force microscopy (FM-KPFM).  The developed method paves the way for 3D-imaging of dielectric constants of composite materials and heterostructures.
steps_raw: We demonstrate a new approach to simultaneously measure the dielectric constants of buried structures and interfaces by combining peak force tapping quantitative Nano-mechanical mapping (PF QNM) and frequency-modulated Kelvin probe force microscopy (FM-KPFM).  The developed method paves the way for 3D-imaging of dielectric constants of composite materials and heterostructures.
keywords: Kelvin Probe Force Microscopy, Subsurface, Dielectric constant
